data.title: China urges dialogue amid rising U.S. trade tensions in new white paper
data.description:
China's State Council Information Office on Wednesday, April 9, released a white paper titled “China's position on some issues concerning China-US economic and trade relations” as renewed trade tensions trigger market downturn. 

Mutually beneficial cooperation can help realize respective development goals:

China and the US have their national realities and are at different stages of development, which might lead to differences and frictions in their economic and trade cooperation, the white paper reads.

No country will meet or satisfy “another country’s unreasonable demands” to the detriment of its reasonable development interests. This, however, does not prevent both sides from seeking possible solutions through equal-footed dialogue, it added.

Revocation of MFN status violates WTO rules:

World Trade Organization (WTO) rules mandate unconditional MFN treatment for all members. Revocation of China’s MFN status undermines China-US economic and trade relations and destabilizes the global economic order.

Any move to revoke China’s MFN status, whether through legislation by the US Congress or based on any existing domestic laws, directly violates US obligations under the WTO, which is a clear manifestation of unilateralism and trade protectionism.

MFN status revocation destabilises global economic order: 

Revoking China’s PNTR status will bring China-US economic and trade relations back to the uncertainty and unpredictability that preceded China’s accession to the WTO in 2001.

China opposes unilateralist and protectionist acts that sabotage multilateral trading system:

China has consistently opposed any unilateralist or protectionist action that could sabotage the multilateral trading system.

“It is hoped that the US will be clearly aware of the possible harm caused by its attempt to revoke China’s MFN status, and work constructively with the overwhelming majority of WTO members in safeguarding a fair and reasonable global economic and trade order and environment.”

Generalization of national security concept hinders China-US economic cooperation:

The US government continues to politicize economic issues on the grounds of national security, and has introduced a variety of policies to hinder economic and trade exchanges with China, with restrictions and sanctions constantly intensifying.

The series of trade and investment restrictions implemented by the US increases compliance costs for enterprises and severely hinders normal China-US economic and trade cooperation.

US abuse of export controls destabilises global supply chain:

The report further states the US has generalised its definition of national security in recent years, used excessive long-arm authority and politicised and weaponised export controls, placing sanctions on foreign industries and enterprises. These behaviours have disturbed normal economic and trade exchanges, as well as the stability of global supply chains.The US suppresses other countries in the name of national security and human rights: 

Unjust U.S. sanctions have had severe consequences for Chinese enterprises affected, such as supply chain disruptions, fund shortages, and loss of partners, substantially infringing on their legitimate rights and interests.

The US has also placed Chinese entities on the Uygur Forced Labour Prevention Act Entity List, claiming ‘forced labour’ issues, despite some companies having automated production or undergoing third-party audits with no evidence found. These restrictions have created a tiered structure within the realm of AI, favouring certain entities while limiting others, including China.Use of Fentanyl as a pretext to impose sanctions is not helping to solve problems: 

The United States increased tariffs across the board on Chinese products exported to the US twice this year and threatened to cancel the duty-free de minimis treatment.  These efforts are ineffective and will not address internal issues in the US. Instead, they will harm China-US economic and trade cooperation, disrupting global trade.

Reciprocal tariffs imposed will damage its own and others' interests: The paper also highlighted that the measure to impose reciprocal tariffs will not address domestic economic issues in the US but will ultimately backfire and make the country a victim of its actions.Conclusion:

China-US cooperation is a must for setting relevant rules and maintaining order, preventing and controlling potential security risks, and ensuring peaceful use rather than misuse of technology.

Following the principles of mutual respect, peaceful coexistence, and win-win cooperation, the two countries can address their respective concerns through equal-footed dialogue and consultation, as “trade wars produce no winners, and protectionism leads up a blind alley.”

China ETFs: (NYSEARCA:KWEB), (NASDAQ:PGJ), (NYSEARCA:FXI), (NYSEARCA:ASHR), (YIN), (NYSEARCA:YANG), (NYSEARCA:GXC), (NYSEARCA:FXP), (KBA), (CXSE), (CNYA), (CHAU), (FLCH), (YXI), (FCA), (KALL), (JCHI)

data.title: Delta Air Lines in charts: Revenue metrics slip while cost pressures rise in Q1
Infosys extends strategic collaboration with AIB
data.description:
• Infosys (NYSE:INFY) said it will extend its strategic collaboration with AIB.
  • The company will take on a renewed and expanded role to support AIB as it further boosts its transformation initiatives.
  • INFY will also help further develop AIB's application landscape and deliver application development and maintenance services.
  • AIB is expected to benefit from increased operational efficiency.

data.title: Ellomay Capital enters into investment agreement with Clal Insurance
data.description:
• Ellomay Capital (NYSE:ELLO) said it has entered into an investment agreement with  Clal Insurance and several of its affiliates for an aggregate investment by Clal of ~€52 million.
  • As part of the deal, Clal and the firm will set up a new Israeli limited partnership in which a unit of Ellomay will be the general partner and Ellomay will hold 51% of the limited partner interests and Clal will hold the rest.
  • The Israeli LP will wholly own a newly founded Luxembourg entity, to which Ellomay’s wholly-owned subsidiary, Ellomay Luxembourg, will transfer shares of seven Italian project companies that hold a solar portfolio in an aggregate capacity of approximately 198 MW.
  • The Italian Solar Portfolio has solar facilities with an aggregate capacity of 38 MW and additional solar facilities with an aggregate capacity of 160 MW. 
  • Under the agreement, the firm and Clal will sign a partners agreement and Ellomay will issue Clal a warrant upon closing.
  • The warrant covers 416,000 ordinary shares of Ellomay, with an exercise price of NIS 69.

data.title: Acer’s March revenue up 6.5% Y/Y
data.description:
Acer (OTC:ACEYY) has reported a 6.5% year-on-year growth in consolidated revenues for March.

Revenues totaled NT$29.28B for the month, with preliminary revenues for Q1 coming at NT$61.42B with 4.4% YoY growth.

Acer Chromebook revenues grew 59.7% YoY in March and 35.2% YoY in Q1. Total revenues from businesses other than personal computers and displays contributed 26.9% of the group's total revenues in March and 31.7% in Q1 with 12.3% growth YoY.

Source: Press Release

data.title: European gas prices drop as tariffs and growth fears rattle broader market
data.description:
European natural gas prices (NGEU:COM) extended their losses to Wednesday as U.S. President Donald Trump's tariffs triggered demand fears and concerns that a global trade war would dent economic growth.

Benchmark futures fell as much as 7.7% on Wednesday, and are down about 40% from a February peak.

At the end of last week, futures prices for natural gas in Europe dropped sharply by over 10% to €35.2 per MWh, according to the ICE exchange. These are the lowest European gas prices in the past six months.

President Trump’s tariffs on global trade partners took effect on Wednesday, including a 104% levy on China that sets the scene for a dramatic escalation between the world’s two largest economies.

Reciprocal tariffs between Washington and Beijing are prompting China’s LNG buyers to resell U.S. cargoes.

Meanwhile, EU members are reportedly negotiating changes to gas storage targets that could soften this year’s goals amid broader concerns over low inventory levels. 

According to Wood Mackenzie, steep increases announced by Trump and retaliation from other countries point to rising costs and weaker energy demand.

U.S. power industry is one of the sectors that is most exposed to tariff impacts, it said.

The Trump administration is aiming to boost production of energy of all kinds, to support the growth of AI and to bring down costs for consumers. But the tariffs could lead to significant cost increases in the power industry supply chain.

"Uncertainty over tariff liabilities in complex supply chains, and over possible future changes in tariff rates, can also be a deterrent to investment," Wood Mackenzie said.

ETFs: (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

Major energy companies in Europe include Shell (SHEL), BP (BP), Engie (OTCPK:ENGIY), EDF,  Vestas (OTCPK:VWDRY), Total Energies (TTE), and National Grid.

data.title: European pharma stocks decline as Trump reiterates tariff plans
data.description:
Shares of major European drugmakers fell Wednesday morning after U.S. President Donald Trump reaffirmed plans to impose a "major" tariff on all pharmaceutical imports.

“We’re going to tariff our pharmaceuticals, and once we do that, they are going to come rushing back into our country, because we’re the big market," Trump said during remarks at a dinner in Washington, D.C.

Europe-listed shares of AstraZeneca fell -5.6%, GSK dipped -4.7%, Roche -5.4%, Sanofi -5.4%, argenx SE -4.8%, UCB SA -7.3% and Novartis -6%.

Nasdaq-listed shares of AstraZeneca (NASDAQ:AZN) were also down 3% premarket, while GSK (NYSE:GSK) fell 3%, Sanofi (NASDAQ:SNY) -2.75% and Novartis (NYSE:NVS) dropped -3.5% in the U.S.

Danish drugmaker Novo Nordisk (NVO) fell 1% premarket in the U.S. and 4.7% in Europe.

A basket of European healthcare stocks (SXDP) dropped 4.6%, hitting its lowest level since October 2022 and leading sectoral losses on the pan-European STOXX 600 index (STOXX), which was down nearly 3%.

While Trump has not specified the timing or scale of the proposed tax hikes on pharmaceutical imports, he stated that the tariffs are intended to encourage drug manufacturers to relocate their operations to the U.S.

The President earlier this month said that several large drugmakers -- Johnson & Johnson (JNJ), Eli Lilly (LLY), and Merck (MRK) -- have made announcements of making manufacturing investments in the U.S. Johnson & Johnson (JNJ), Eli Lilly (LLY), and Merck (MRK) fell 0.69%, 0.86% and 1.29%, respectively.

data.title: Tariffs wars: China slams Trump's 104% levies, vows retaliation
